title_ar: أذكار الصباح والمساء وما بعد الصلاة
content_en:
Among the most frequently used sections of Al-Adhkar are those dealing with the morning and evening remembrances — the adhkar that the Prophet prescribed for the two bookends of the day. An-Nawawi gathered under these headings the full range of supplications and formulas that the prophetic tradition recommends for these pivotal moments: the formulas of glorification, praise, and declaration of God's oneness and greatness that the Prophet repeated in specific numbers, the supplications for protection from harm, disease, and the evil eye, and the longer formulas that invoke God's mercy and blessings on the day ahead or the night to follow.

The morning adhkar in Al-Adhkar have been particularly influential in shaping the devotional practice of Muslims across the world. The practice of reciting these remembrances after the Fajr prayer has been a feature of Muslim daily life in all the major Islamic cultures, and an-Nawawi's organization of them into a clear, comprehensive sequence made Al-Adhkar the standard reference for scholars and educators seeking to teach this practice. The translations of these sections into virtually every major language spoken by Muslims reflect their practical importance.

The remembrances that follow the five daily prayers receive extensive treatment in Al-Adhkar. An-Nawawi gathered the full range of supplications and formulas that the Prophet prescribed for after-prayer practice: the specific numbers of glorifications, praises, and declarations of God's greatness; the recitation of the Throne Verse; and the various supplications that the Prophet taught his Companions to recite after completing the prayer. The organization and presentation of this material in Al-Adhkar has made it the standard reference for the after-prayer adhkar in the Shafi'i tradition and beyond.

The sections on the night prayer and the remembrances before sleep are among the most spiritually resonant in the book. An-Nawawi gathered the prophetic supplications for lying down, the formulas of remembrance that the Prophet prescribed for the transition from wakefulness to sleep, and the prayers for protection during the night. These sections have made Al-Adhkar a bedside companion for generations of Muslim believers.
